tags.name: viewModel
locale
definitions
tags.description:
Forms, sections, and components defined in `/definitions` can be reused across pages.

`"$ref"` is a link to a definition file in `/definitions` folder. It helps you keep common UI fragments in one place and reuse them across multiple pages.

How it works:
- Create a JSON snippet under `/definitions`.
- Reference it from a page configuration via `"$ref": "#/path/to/definition"`.
- When the page is rendered, the referenced definition is merged into the object that contains `$ref`.

Notes:
- The `$ref` value must be a literal string (placeholders are not resolved inside `$ref`).
- If a definition cannot be resolved, `$ref` is kept as-is.
- If a definition is resolved, `$ref` is removed.
- If both the definition and the object containing `$ref` define the same field, the definition value wins.
- Best practice: avoid putting instance-specific fields (like `id`) into definitions unless you want them fixed everywhere.

**Example**

Folder structure:

``` javascript
/definitions
  info
  test
  next
```

The `$ref` path points to a file (or nested file path) under `/definitions`.

For nested folders, the path works the same way:

```javascript
/definitions/common/buttons/next
```

is referenced as:

```javascript
{ "$ref": "#/common/buttons/next" }
```

Form reference:

``` javascript
  {
    "id": "info_test",
    "type": "body",
    "$ref": "#/info"
  }
```

Section reference:

``` javascript
  {
    "id": "info_test",
    "type": "body",
    "sections": [
      {
        "id": "common",
        "$ref": "#/test"
      }
    ]
  }
```

Button reference:

``` javascript
  {
    "class": "button",
    "$ref": "#/next"
  }
```

| | | [iurl=log_in]Internal link[/iurl] | Internal link | | [url=https://simulator.company] External link[/url] | External link | | [div]New line[/div] |
New line

tags.name: styles
realtime
tags.description:
The **Pages Realtime API** allows you to push UI updates to users' browsers in real time, without requiring any user interaction (such as clicking a button or submitting a form).

This is useful when your Corezoid business logic needs to notify the user, update form values, or change component visibility based on an external event — for example, after a background process completes, a payment is confirmed, or another user performs an action.

### How it works

In the standard flow, the user interacts with the page (clicks a button), and the Corezoid process returns a response with `changes`, `notifications`, or `ctrl` commands. The page updates accordingly.

With the Realtime API, a Corezoid process can send those same `changes`, `notifications`, and `ctrl` commands **at any time** — without waiting for user interaction. The updates are delivered to the user's browser via WebSocket and applied immediately.

| Flow | Description |
|------|-------------|
| **Standard** | User clicks button → Corezoid process → response with changes |
| **Realtime** | Corezoid process → HTTP call to Realtime API → WebSocket → page updates |

The `data` object in the request body uses the same format as the `send` response with code `200`. All the same properties are supported: `visibility`, `value`, `error`, `errorMsg`, `styleClass` and any other component property.

**Important:** unlike `send` response changes, `formId` is **required** for every item in `changes` array.

### Targeting

The `receivers` array determines **which users** receive the update. Each receiver is identified by `userId` — the numeric user ID from `sessionData.userInfo.id`.

The `tabId` field is **optional** and determines **which browser tab** receives the update. When omitted, all open tabs displaying the target page receive the update (broadcast mode). When provided, only the tab with the matching `tabId` processes the update — all other tabs ignore it.

Both values are available in the `sessionData` object that your Corezoid process receives with every `get` and `send` request:

- **`userId`** — `sessionData.userInfo.id`
- **`tabId`** — `sessionData.tabId`

You can store these values in your Corezoid process state and use them later when calling the Realtime API.

tags.name: page
tags.description:
Description

All content is located on the page. Each page is a folder with a unique name and two files inside: "config" and "locale". The config file is a JSON file with two main properties: "grid" and "forms". In the "grid" property, we can adjust the arrangement of elements on the page. There are two options for placing content: "two_column" and "one_column". In the locale file, we can set up localization for the page.

Additionally, for each page it is possible to add a "style" file via the context menu. This file allows the user to define custom styles that apply only to this specific page.

Two column grid

One column grid

Configuration

tags.name: form
tags.description:
Description

The form is filled with sections, and those, in turn, with components. Forms, sections, and components have the "visibility" property, which allows for flexible layout customization. Forms are used to send a request to Corezoid when a button is clicked, provided all mandatory fields are filled and all fields are checked. Forms also offer the ability to further customize the arrangement of sections using the "grid" property. There are two types: "two_column" and "one_column".

Two column grid

One column grid

Configuration

tags.name: stepper
tags.description:
Description

The “Stepper” component enables exploring and switching between different views. The Stepper organizes and allows navigation between related groups of content that are located at the same hierarchy level. It provides the same functionality as the “Tab” component, however with another visualization.

Default stepper

Default stepper is used for desktop devices

Mobile stepper

Mobile stepper is used for mobile devices

Example

You want the sidebar stepper to be hidden on mobile devices and the main layout stepper to be visible on mobile devices.

By setting `mobileVisible: false` for the sidebar stepper and `mobileVisible: true` for the main layout stepper, you are configuring the behavior so that the sidebar stepper will be hidden on mobile devices and the main layout stepper will be visible on mobile devices.This setup will result in an auto-closing sidebar stepper (hidden on mobile) and an auto-opening stepper on the main layout (visible on mobile).

Desktop layout

Mobile layout

Configuration

tags.name: button
tags.description:
Description

Buttons allow users to take actions and make choices with a single tap.
Buttons communicate the actions that users can perform. They are usually placed throughout your user interface in areas such as:

- Modal windows
- Forms
- Cards

Default button

Default buttons are of high emphasis and are distinguished by their use of elevation and fill. They contain actions that are essential to your app.

Default buttons show a spinner while their /send request is being processed. They also reflect form validation state automatically: if a visible required field in the form is empty or has an error, default buttons are switched to disabled. When all required fields are valid, they are switched back to visible.

Text button

Text buttons are commonly used for less prominent actions, including those found in dialogs and cards. In cards, text buttons assist in maintaining focus on the card's content.

Secondary button

Secondary buttons are of medium emphasis. They represent actions that are significant but not the primary action within an app.

Secondary buttons show a spinner while their /send request is being processed. Unlike default buttons, they do not automatically switch to disabled based on required field validation.

Tertiary button

Tertiary buttons are low-emphasis buttons that represent actions of minimal importance within an app.

They serve as a less prominent option compared to other button types.

Quaternary button

Quaternary buttons are also low-emphasis buttons, designed for actions of even lesser importance in an app.

They serve as a straight alternative to tertiary buttons, providing an even lower level of emphasis in comparison to secondary or text buttons.

Quinary button

Quinary buttons are used when an action needs a minimalist button design with no background at all, including on hover. Unlike text buttons, quinary buttons keep the background transparent in all interactive states.

Even though the background is not visible, the interactive area of a quinary button has the same size as other button types.

Error button

Error buttons are used for destructive, critical, or error-related actions that need to stand apart from regular actions.

Button with context menu

Menus display a list of choices on temporary surfaces. A menu displays a list of choices on a temporary surface. It appears when the user interacts with a button.

Configuration

Description

Sliders reflect a range of values along a bar, from which users may select a single value. They are ideal for adjusting settings such as volume, brightness, or applying image filters.

Default slider

The "Default slider" component allows users to make selections from a range of values.

SkillBar slider

The "SkillBar slider" component allows users to show the progress from a range of values. There is no any handles, it`s used only for view.

Checkboxes allow the user to select one or more items from a set. Checkboxes can be used to turn an option on or off.

Labels allow the user to show information to user.

A divider is a thin line that groups content in lists and layouts. Dividers separate content into clear groups.

Configuration

tags.name: image

Description

“Image” allows the user to display images to other users.

Configuration

tags.name: file

Description

The "File" component allows the user to view a file by clicking a button. After clicking, a modal window will open displaying the file specified in the configuration.

Configuration

Description

The "Carousel" component allows the user to display an array of files. When clicking on an icon representing a file, the same modal window as the one triggered by the "File" component will appear, displaying the selected file.

The "Copy" icon allows the user to copy information into the clipboard.

The "Upload" component allows the user to upload files to your app. After clicking, the system file explorer will appear, allowing the user to choose a file to upload.

Configuration

tags.name: attachment

Attachment allow user to upload multiply files to your app. After click the system file explorer will appear, where user can choose file to upload or use drag&drop

tags.name: widget
tags.description:
Description

We have integrated with Twilio video and Onfido.

Default widget

In default mode, the "Widget" component embeds the provided URL inside an HTML iframe. #### Messaging In default mode, the widget is an iframe that loads any URL you provide. It can be a plain HTML page (for example, a help panel or a static dashboard) or a fully interactive mini-app (for example, a calendar picker, a custom search UI, a signature pad, or a file uploader). If your iframe content is static, it can live completely independently. But if you want it to behave like a real form control inside a script, it needs a way to exchange data with the host page. Scripts can define both **configuration** and **state** for the widget: - Configuration is passed via `extra.config` and can contain any settings you need (theme, feature flags, API endpoints, allowed file types, etc.). - State is the widget `value`: it is part of the current form, can be submitted to the backend, and can be updated by the backend via `changes`. To support these scenarios across domains, the parent page implements a small [postMessage](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Window/postMessage) interface. The widget can request its current value/config (and even the full form data), and can send commands back (update value, upload files, resize, toggle loading, show notifications). You can implement the widget side with plain JavaScript — no knowledge of internal implementation details is required. #### Transport Communication uses the `postMessage` interface. The parent script Widget component listens and responds only to its iframe events. ```javascript const APP_NAME = 'scPostMessage'; // Inside widget iframe window.addEventListener('message', (event) => { const { data } = event; if (!data || data.appName !== APP_NAME) return; const { type, payload, result, error } = data; // Your events handling here }); // Send new value from widget window.parent.postMessage( { appName: APP_NAME, type: 'UPDATE_VALUE', payload: { value: 'new value' }, }, '*' ); ``` Your widget is allowed to send a message to the parent window with `appName = scPostMessage`, `type` (see below), and (optionally) `payload` object. If the event expects any request parameter, it should be placed **inside the payload object**. If the event expects a response, the parent sends back another message that contains the original request fields and adds `result` on success or `error = true` on failure. It is a good practice to add a correlation field to every request (for example `requestId`, `timestamp`, or `ref`) so the widget can reliably match async responses to the corresponding request. The parent echoes request fields back in the response. ```javascript // Request (widget → parent) { appName: 'scPostMessage', timestamp: Date.now(), // optional type: 'REQUEST_VALUE', payload: { /* request parameters (optional) */ } } // Response (parent → widget) { appName: 'scPostMessage', timestamp: 1710000000000, type: 'REQUEST_VALUE', /* ...original request fields... */ result: '...', // on success // error: true // on error } ``` #### Widget → Parent events | Type | Payload keys | Effect | | --- | --- | --- | | UPDATE_VALUE | value (required, any) | Sets the widget form value to payload.value. This value becomes part of the current form and will be sent to the backend on submit (and may be submitted immediately if the widget is configured with submitOnChange). Use this event to send a clean value (see Legacy behavior). | | REQUEST_VALUE | – | Parent replies with current value in result. | | REQUEST_CONFIG | – | Parent replies with current config (extra.config). | | REQUEST_FORM_DATA | – | Parent replies with full form data object. | | UPLOAD_FILES | files (required, array of File)
api (object, { uploadUrl, downloadUrl, auth })
compressionLevel (optional) | Uploads files via the host script; reply returns uploaded file descriptors in result or error. The descriptor format matches the Upload component value (see Upload). | | RESIZE_WIDGET | width
height | By default, the host script controls iframe sizing. Send this event to ask the parent script to resize the iframe to the specified dimensions (for example, to fit your widget content). Numbers are treated as pixels. | | SET_LOADING | isLoading (required, boolean) | Toggles widget loading overlay in the parent script UI. | | SEND_NOTIFICATION | title
id
helperText
expire
type | Shows a generic notification using the parent script notification system. | | SEND_CUSTOM_EVENT | any | Sends a custom event to the host. The host forwards it to Corezoid as a form submit action with buttonId equal to the widget id and buttonData containing the event object. This event **does not** change the widget `value`. | | Any other type (custom event) | any | If the iframe sends a message with an unknown `type`, the host treats it as a custom event. By default, the host forwards it to Corezoid as a form submit action with buttonId equal to the widget id and buttonData containing the event object, and it does not change the widget `value`. If `extra.allowedEvents` is set, only listed custom types are accepted; others are ignored. If `extra.legacyEventsHandling` is true, custom events are treated as widget `value` updates (legacy). | **Legacy behavior:** Some older widgets send messages without a type. In this case, the host treats the whole message as a value update and stores the entire event object as the widget value. For custom events (unknown type values), you can enable the same legacy “value update” behavior by setting `extra.legacyEventsHandling` to true; otherwise custom events are forwarded as submit actions (the same way as `SEND_CUSTOM_EVENT` does) and do not change the widget value. #### Parent → Widget events On widget load the parent sends `UPDATE_VALUE` and `UPDATE_CONFIG` if `value` or `extra.config` are set in the widget JSON. Later script/backend updates arrive the same way. | Type |
Keys
| When sent | | --- | --- | --- | | UPDATE_VALUE | value(any) | On iframe load (if set) and when value changes in script/backend. | | UPDATE_CONFIG | config(any) | On iframe load (if extra.config set) and when config changes. | | Some of Widget → Parent events | result(any)
error(boolean) | For some request-type events, the parent sends a separate response message that echoes the request fields and adds result on success or error = true on failure. |

Web comments widget

Web comments allow users to add commentaries on a page.

Follow the next steps to enable this feature:

1. Go to Simulator.company
2. Select "Communications"
3. Choose "Comments Widget"
4. Create Comments Widget actor
5. Copy actorId
6. Go to script
7. Insert the actorId into the `ctrlSetting.webComments.{{actorId}}` widget section.

Configuration

tags.name: edit
tags.description: ### Description `Edit` is an input field used in forms and dialogs. It allows users to enter and edit text, supports multiple formats: text, numbers, email, password, multiline text, date, and color picker. ### Text edit Free-form text input. ### Int edit Integer-only input (non-digit characters are ignored). The value is stored as a number. ### Float edit Floating-point number input. ### Phone edit Phone number input. ### Email edit Email input. Validates the email format. ### Password edit Password input (characters are hidden). ### Multiline edit Multi-line text area. Configure the number of visible rows using `extra.lineNumbers`. ### Color Picker edit Color picker input. Uses a popover selector UI. ### Date edit Date (or date range) input. Uses a calendar popover selector UI. ### Configuration

tags.name: select
tags.description:
Description

Select components are used for collecting user-provided information from a list of options.

Default select

Menus are positioned under their emitting elements, unless they are located close to the bottom of the viewport.

Autocomplete select

The autocomplete select is a regular text input that is enhanced with a panel of suggested options.

Extended select

Select options support additional properties including avatars, icons, badges, and tooltips. These properties are rendered both in the dropdown menu and in the field displaying the selected option.

Configuration

tags.name: multiselect
tags.description:
Description

The “Multiselect” component can manage multiple selections simultaneously.

Configuration

tags.name: radio
tags.description:
Description

The “Radio” component enables the user to select a single option from a set. Radio buttons are used when the user should be able to view all available options

Configuration

tags.name: phone
tags.description:
Description

The "Phone Edit" component offers users a user-friendly form for inputting phone numbers.

Configuration

tags.name: tab
tags.description:
Description

Tabs provide a convenient way to explore and switch between various views. They help organize and facilitate navigation between groups of related content that exist at the same hierarchical level.

Configuration

tags.name: comments
tags.description:
Description

Component comments allow user to show comments with user name, description, text, date of comment and location.

Configuration

tags.name: table
tags.description:
Description

Tables present datasets in an organized format that can be fully customized. They showcase data in a manner that's easy to scan, so that users can look for patterns and insights. Tables can be seamlessly integrated into primary content, such as modal windows, and may include:

- Corresponding visual representations
- Navigation options
- Tools for querying and manipulating data

Default table

A simple example with no frills.

Radio table

Radio table allows the user to select one row from a table.

Check table

Checkboxes allow the user to select one or more rows from a table.

Group table

Group tables allow the user to group table rows based on common attributes.

Configuration

tags.name: otp
tags.description:
Description

The "OTP" component allows users to input a one-time password (OTP).

Configuration

tags.name: signature
tags.description:
Description

The "Signature" component allows users to create handwritten signatures and submit them as part of a form.

Configuration

tags.name: mainMenu
tags.description:
### Description The main menu renders a hierarchical navigation tree that can highlight the active path, display custom badges or icons, and submit actions back to the form when users select an item. Inline branches expand directly in the list, while deeper levels open a contextual pop-up to keep navigation compact. #### Collapsed sidebar mode When embedding the main menu into a collapsible sidebar, you can enable `extra.collapsed`. - The first level becomes an icon-only vertical stack (labels and right-side badges are hidden visually). - Deeper levels are always opened through popovers (equivalent to `maxInlineDepth = 1`). - Top-level leaf items show a tooltip on hover. The tooltip text is the item title. If the item has a badge, the badge text is appended in parentheses. - If a top-level item has no icon configured, a default `circle_filled` icon is used in collapsed mode. - A plain `collapsed` CSS class is added to the root element so you can target this state.
#### Corezoid request payload The component state lives in `value`. It always carries the current `activeId` and `expandedIds` array, which are sent to Corezoid as part of the form state on each update. Every interaction with the main menu may trigger a `/send` request to Corezoid. The `buttonId` field contains the menu id, while `buttonData` describes the event: ```json { "buttonId": "mainMenu", "buttonData": { "action": "select", "id": "flows", // id of the clicked item "path": ["operations", "automation", "flows"], "meta": // optional meta payload provided in the item config } } ``` If `submitOnBranchToggle` is enabled, expanding/collapsing a branch sends: ```json { "buttonId": "mainMenu", "buttonData": { "action": "expand" | "collapse", "id": "automation", "path": ["operations", "automation"], // full path to the branch "meta": // optional meta payload provided in the item config } } ``` #### postMessage navigation mode By default, every menu interaction triggers a `/send` request to Corezoid. When `extra.postMessageTargetId` is set to the `id` of a widget on the page, the component switches to **postMessage mode**: instead of sending a form submit, it posts a message directly to the widget's iframe. The message has the following structure: ```json { "appName": "scPostMessage", "type": "PM_MENU_NAVIGATE", "payload": { "id": "flows", "action": "select", "path": ["operations", "automation", "flows"], "meta": { "url": "/automation/flows" }, "menuId": "mainMenu" } } ``` - `menuId` — the `id` of the MainMenu component that sent the message. - The rest of the `payload` fields match the standard `buttonData` structure described above. This mode is useful when the menu drives navigation inside an embedded widget without a server round-trip. > **Note:** In postMessage mode, menu clicks do not trigger a form submit to Corezoid. The component `value` (`activeId`, `expandedIds`) is still updated locally and will be included in the form data when the form is submitted by other means. ### Configuration

tags.name: timer
tags.description: ### Description A display-only component that shows a running timer. It can count up (stopwatch) or count down (countdown) and supports multiple display formats. The timer starts automatically when the page loads. Although the component has a `value` property, it is not included in the form data sent to Corezoid on submit. The timer also does not trigger `submitOnChange` — it is purely visual. #### Dynamic updates The timer re-anchors when `value`, `type`, or `mode` are changed via `changes` (including realtime). This allows you to reset or restart the timer from the server at any time. The timer pauses automatically when `visibility` is set to `hidden` and resumes when made visible again. ### Configuration

post.description: The request is sent to the corezoid process before the page is loaded. The corezoid process decides whether to display the requested page or to redirect the user to another page.  Optionally, data for dynamic injection into the page body can be sent in the response.

timeZoneOffset.description: browser timezone offset from UTC, in minutes

post.description: The request is sent to the Corezoid process when a button is clicked or when interacting with a component which submitOnChange property is set to true. The request's body contains all information about the component that initiated the request and the values of all fields in the current form. The Corezoid process makes decisions regarding changes on the existing page, displaying notifications, or redirecting the user to another page.

viewModel.note: viewModel is optional. Used to resolve {{...}} placeholders that are not present in each contentLoop item (e.g. {{currency}}).

timeZoneOffset.description: browser timezone offset from UTC, in minutes

buttonData.description: additional info regarding the action that triggered the request

post.description: Push UI updates to users' browsers in real time via WebSocket, without requiring any user interaction. The request delivers changes, notifications, query parameters and widget commands to target users' browsers immediately.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Authentication header in format `Bearer {apiKey}`. `apiKey` is a Simulator workspace access key generated in the Corezoid account interface at account.corezoid.com.

language.description: Language used to resolve `[[...]]` locale keys in this payload

query.description: Optional page query replacement. CDU updates target page query only when `data.query` is a JSON object. Pass `{}` to clear current page query.

changes.description: Incremental UI updates applied to components, sections, or forms

notifications.description: Toast messages displayed to the user

ctrl.title: Array of widget commands
ctrl.description: Commands sent to the parent window (for widget/iframe scenarios)

receivers.description: Target users who will receive the update

tabId.description: Optional. Target a specific browser tab. When omitted, all tabs receive the update (broadcast mode). When provided, only the tab with matching tabId processes the update.

notifications.title: System maintenance
notifications.helperText: The system will be unavailable from 22:00 to 23:00 UTC

header.description: The header of the sidebar if it is not specified, the default logo will be used

expire.title: If 'auto', notification will be hidden in 5 sec

styleClass.description: css class that is defined in script style files

language.description: Base locale for the page. Used to resolve `[[...]]` locale keys and by components such as [Edit (date)](#date-edit) to automatically format month names, weekday labels, e.t.c. Accepts any valid BCP 47 language tag (e.g. `en`, `uk`, `en-GB`, `ru`). Can be overridden at runtime by the `language` field in a [/get](#operation/getPage) or `205 + viewModel` [/send](#operation/sendForm) request responses.

allOf.description:
Component visibility :
  * visible - component is displayed on the page, can be used
  * hidden -  component is not displayed on the page
  * disabled - component is displayed, but cannot be used

type.description:
Visualization type :
  * body - as part of the body
  * block - with the gray background
  * modal - as a modal window
  * float - as a draggable floating panel

header.title: Components that will be displayed without background

modalHeader.title: In modal type will display these components instead default header

modalCloseConfirmText.description: When set to a non-empty string, a browser confirm dialog with this text is shown before closing a `modal` or `float` section. If empty or omitted, the section closes immediately without confirmation.
modalCloseConfirmText.example: You have unsaved changes. Are you sure you want to close?

isResizable.description: Allows the user to resize a float section by dragging its edges. Only applies when type is `float`.

modalSize.description: Controls the size of the modal window

contentLoop.title: An array of objects. The content will be drawn in a loop. The object will be used for dynamic substitutions through {{}}

items.title: Variables that will be replaced in page configuration {{key}}

sortable.description:
Enables drag-and-drop reordering of `contentLoop` items within the section. Only takes effect when `contentLoop` is present.

When enabled, each `contentLoop` iteration is wrapped in a card-like container with a drag handle on the left side. The user can grab the handle and drag items to rearrange their order within the section.

**Item IDs.** When `sortable` is enabled, the `id` field in each `contentLoop` object is reserved to identify the draggable item. If `id` is not provided, a fallback `{sectionId}-cl-{index}` (zero-based) will be used.

**Reorder event.** After the user drops an item into a new position, a form submit event is sent to Corezoid on behalf of the section:
- `buttonId` is set to the section `id`.
- `buttonData` contains:
  ```json
  {
    "action": "reorder",
    "value": ["item-id-1", "item-id-2", "item-id-3"]
  }
  ```
  where `value` is an array of these item IDs in the new order from top to bottom.

content.title: Components that will be displayed on the gray background

type.description:
Visual button type.

A CDU form is the page block that groups sections, fields, and action components. When a button submits, the form data is sent to /send.

`default` and `secondary` buttons show a spinner while their /send request is being processed.
Only `default` buttons can automatically reflect the validation state of the surrounding form. If any visible **required** field is empty or has `error = true`, the button is switched to `visibility = disabled`; when there are no such errors, it is switched back to `visibility = visible`.

url.description: URL to navigate to on button click. When set, the button opens the URL instead of submitting an event to Corezoid.

target.description: Works with `url`. Set to `_blank` to open the URL in a new tab.

action.title: Specific action with internal API
action.description: Runs a built-in client action instead of the default button submit behavior.

rounded.description: If true, renders the button with rounded corners.

mobileVisible.title: Show component only on small (mobile/tablet) resolutions
mobileVisible.description: If true, shows the button only on small resolutions and hides it on desktop.

request.title: Request that will be triggered onClick before /send
request.description: Sends the configured HTTP request first. After it completes, the button submits the form event to Corezoid.

method.description: HTTP method used for the request.

autoSubmit.title: Auto submit options
autoSubmit.description:
Periodically submits the button while it is mounted.

Auto submit continues when the button is disabled, pauses and resets while hidden, and pauses while the button is already submitting.

options.description: Menu options shown when the button is clicked. Selecting an option submits the form with the selected value, unless the option action is `logout`.

action.description: Runs a built-in client action when this menu option is selected. If omitted, selecting the option submits the form with `buttonData.value` equal to the option value.

mobileVisible.title: Show component only on small (mobile/tablet) resolutions

resettable.title: If true, reset button will be displayed

submitOnScroll.title: If true, /send request will be triggered on scroll

icon.description: Optional icon shown to the left of the option label.

tooltip.description: Tooltip displays informative text when users hover over an option. The text appears after 1.5 seconds.

avatar.description: Optional circular avatar shown on the far left. If the image is unavailable, the initials of the first two words of the option title are used instead. Both avatar and icon can be shown together.

badge.description: Badge value shown on the far right.

styleClass.description: Extra CSS class applied to the option. When this option is selected, the same class is added to the Select component.

size.description: Size of the toggle switch (small, medium, large)

submitOnChange.description: if true, changes of the element's value will trigger /send request.

measure.description: Extra text that will be shown after the min/max value

dots.description: If true, the slider will be displayed with dots

id.description: Identifier of the component (must be unique within the scope of the form)

row.description: Row number. Components with the same row number will be aligned in one row

w.description: Relative width of the component in the row (%). If not specified, the width will be the same for all components in the row

type.description:
Visualization type :
  * default - list of options only
  * preview - the selected option will be displayed in a preview

fileSrc.title: source
fileSrc.description: For external usage, input the link to your file into this field.

fileName.title: identifier
fileName.description: For internal usage, input the file name from simulator.company into this field.

type.description:
Component type :
  * default - upload component with preview, file will be selected from the hard disk
  * webcam - upload component with preview, screenshot will be made from webcam

errorMsgInvalidFileType.title: Error message shown when the file type is invalid.

errorMsgMinImageSize.title: Error message shown when the file size < minSize

errorMsgMaxImageSize.title: Error message shown when the file size > maxSize

screenshotFormat.title: screenshot format
screenshotFormat.description: Shows the format of the image to be returned in webcam type.

width.title: width
width.description: The width of the photo to configure.

height.title: height
height.description: The height of the photo to configure.

mirrored.title: mirrored
mirrored.description: If true, the image will be mirrored.

value.description: Widget value (part of the current form state). The format is defined by your widget implementation.

allowFullScreen.description: Enables fullscreen mode inside the iframe (adds the `allowFullScreen` attribute).

allow.description: Sets the iframe `allow` attribute (permissions policy), e.g. to grant access to camera/microphone.

config.description: Optional widget configuration (arbitrary JSON) that is delivered to the iframe on initialization and on changes.

allowedEvents.description:
Optional allowlist for custom events sent from the iframe.

- If the key is not provided, any custom event type is allowed.
- If an empty array is provided, custom events are ignored.
- If a non-empty array is provided, only listed custom event types are allowed; others are ignored.

Notes:
- This setting applies to custom events (unknown `type` values), not to built-in widget messages such as `UPDATE_VALUE`.
- How an allowed custom event is handled depends on `legacyEventsHandling` (forwarded by default, stored as `value` in legacy mode).

legacyEventsHandling.description: Legacy mode for custom (unknown) events. - `false` (default): custom events are forwarded to Corezoid as a form submit action with buttonId equal to the widget id and buttonData containing the event object. Custom events do not change the widget `value`. - `true`: custom events are treated as value updates (the widget `value` becomes the whole event object). Notes: - If `allowedEvents` is set, it still acts as an allowlist for which custom event types are accepted.

roomName.description: Twilio Video room name to connect to.

emptyRoomLabel.description: Text shown when you are connected, but there are no remote participants yet.

disconnectButtonVisibility.description: Show or hide the disconnect button.

initialCameraState.description: If true, camera is initially enabled.

initialMicrophoneState.description: If true, microphone is initially enabled.

sdkVersion.description: Onfido Web SDK version used to load the library (for example, `v14`).

initOptions.description:
Onfido Web SDK initialization options. See Onfido docs:

https://documentation.onfido.com/sdk/web/#initialization-options

phone.description:
Phone number to call.

Use digits only (the `+` sign is added automatically).

immediateCall.description: Reserved for future use (currently has no effect).

queueArnIsRequired.example: Queue ARN is required to connect

title.description: This value is used as a title for the copy chip

allOf.description: Tooltip displays informative text when users hover over an element.

value.description: Value that will be copied to the clipboard

allOf.description: if true, clicking the copy element will first submit form data to Corezoid, wait for response, then copy the current value to clipboard. If the server response updated the value, the new value will be copied. If false, copies current value immediately without server interaction.

notificationText.description: Text that will be shown in the notification, if there is no text, the notification will not be shown

type.description: With "int" type sets the keyboard to numeric input mode on mobile devices.

regexp.title: Regular expression for validation of the countryCode+number

autoScroll.description: When true, the component automatically scrolls to the last message on each update. Set to false to keep the current scroll position.

additionalProperties.title: value of the property : visibility, title, options, value, etc.

skipSubmitOnChange.title: skipSubmitOnChange boolean flag
skipSubmitOnChange.description: Suppress auto-submit when pushing a value to a component with submitOnChange

changeRules.title: Change rules for array properties
changeRules.description: Keys are names of array-type component properties (e.g. options). Each key defines how the incoming array value is merged with the existing one.

namespace.title: Widget namespace
namespace.description: Target namespace. Use `webWidget` for the embeddable Simulator widget — a Smart Form embedded inside the widget can send commands to control the widget itself (e.g. open, close).

language.description: Language used to resolve `[[...]]` locale keys in this response.

viewModel.title: Variables that will be replaced in the page configuration {{key}}

language.title: Page locale
language.description: Language used to resolve `[[...]]` locale keys. Overrides the [page config](#tag/page) `language` value.

nextPage.title: Next page id. Browser will be redirected.

viewModel.title: Variables that will be replaced in page configuration {{key}}

language.title: Page locale
language.description: Language used to resolve `[[...]]` locale keys. Overrides the [page config](#tag/page) `language` value.

remindDate.description: Unix timestamp in seconds. Used when reminders are enabled (`extra.remind` is true).

timeZoneOffset.description:
Time zone offset in minutes.

Example: UTC+2 is stored as `-120`.

sendInvite.description: Whether a calendar invite should be sent (set by the user when `extra.sendInvite` is enabled).

time.description: If true, the user can select time (hours and minutes) in addition to the date.

timeZone.description: If true, the user can select a time zone. The selected value is stored in `value.timeZoneOffset`.

remind.description:
If true, the calendar shows a reminder selector so the user can choose when to be reminded.

The selected reminder is stored in `value.remindDate` as an absolute Unix timestamp (seconds) of the reminder moment. Example: if `startDate` is `1704067200`, then "10 minutes before" is stored as `1704066600`.

dateRange.description: If true, the user can select an end date and create a date range (`startDate` and `endDate`).

isStatic.description:
Static date mode.

Use this mode when you need a date without time (a "day", not a specific moment).

- Displays the date in locale-aware date-only format (e.g. `25 November 2025` by default). `dateFormat`, `time`, `timeZone`, and `dateRange` are ignored.
- The stored value is normalized to the start of the selected day in UTC (`timeZoneOffset = 0`).

sendInvite.description:
If true, the calendar shows a "Calendar invite" toggle.

The selected value is stored in `value.sendInvite`.

This can be used by the backend/integration to decide whether to send a calendar invitation for the selected date (for example, an email with an attached calendar event).

isoDays.description: If true, the week always starts on Monday and the date input placeholder is set to `YYYY-MM-DD` (ISO 8601). If false, both the first day of the week and the input placeholder are determined from the [page config](#tag/page) `language` property (e.g. Sunday for `en` or `en-US`, Monday for `uk` or `en-GB`). > **Note:** When `language` is not set, the calendar defaults to `en-GB` (Monday). Setting `language: "en"` without `isoDays: true` will switch the week to start on Sunday, because `en` without a region code resolves to US English where Sunday is the first day of the week.

dateFormat.description:
Format pattern for the selected date displayed in the Edit input field. Does not affect the calendar popup itself.

Build the format using supported tokens and any literal characters (such as separators or text).
Literal characters are kept as-is and appear in the displayed value unchanged.

Supported tokens:
- `YYYY` year
- `MM` month number (01-12)
- `DD` day of month (01-31)
- `HH` hours
- `mm` minutes
- `ss` seconds
- `AMPM` AM/PM
- `M` month name (January, February, ...)
- `m` short month name (Jan, Feb, ...)

> **Note:** `M` and `m` tokens are automatically localized based on the page [language](#tag/page) or the `language` field in the [/get](#operation/getPage) response.

Examples:
- `YYYY.MM.DD`
- `DD M YYYY`
- `DD m YYYY`
- `DD M YYYY, HH:mm`
- `DD M HH:mm`
- `DD m HH:mm`
- `m DD, YYYY; HH:mm`
- `M DD, YYYY; HH:mm`
- `DD.MM.YY`
- `HH:mm:ss`
- `HH:mm`
- `YYYY/MM/DD HH:mm:ss`

Ignored when `isStatic: true`.

labels.description:
Custom labels for the calendar UI elements.

Use this to override default English labels with translated or custom text.
When `language` property is set on the [Page](#tag/page), the calendar already localizes month names, weekdays, and durations automatically. Labels override any remaining UI strings.

confirmDate.description: Confirm button label when time is not shown.

confirmDateAndTime.description: Confirm button label when time is shown.

calendarInvite.description: Toggle label for the "send calendar invite" option (see `extra.sendInvite`).

timezone.description: Dropdown label for the time zone selector (see `extra.timeZone`).

remind.description: Dropdown label for the reminder selector (see `extra.remind`).

remindOptions.description: Labels for reminder options. Duration-based options (e.g. "5 minutes before") are generated using the `before` template and the page config `language` for duration formatting.

before.description: Template for duration-based items. `{time}` is replaced with the formatted duration.

minDate.description: Minimal selectable date (Unix timestamp in seconds).

maxDate.description: Maximal selectable date (Unix timestamp in seconds).

value.description:
HEX color value.

Format:
- When `extra.colorPicker.disableAlpha: true` (default), the value is stored as `#RRGGBB`.
- When `extra.colorPicker.disableAlpha: false`, the value may include alpha and be stored as `#RRGGBBAA`.

allOf.description: If set to true, this will impact the visibility of the submit button on this form. “Labels” allows the user to display information to other users.
If true, the field must not be empty. An empty value is treated as an error.
If true, the field is shown in the error state.
Error message. Will be visible when component's 'error' flag is set to true
Helper text shown below the field when `error = true`.
Help message. Will be visible when component's 'error' flag is set to false
allOf.example: This information is needed to make our product better

allOf.description: Helper text shown below the field when there is no error.
If true, the form will be submitted automatically after the value changes.

submitOnEnter.description: If true, the Enter key will submit the form (single-line fields only).

tooltip.description: Tooltip text shown near the title as an info icon and appears on hover.

resettable.description:
If true, a reset (clear) button will be displayed.

Default is `true` for `type: date`, otherwise `false`.

maskOptions.description:
Additional settings for `mask`.

Use these options to control how the placeholder looks and behaves.

placeholderChar.description: The character used for the mask placeholder.

lazy.description: If `false`, the placeholder is always visible (even when the field is empty).

mask.description: Use `mask` to guide and format user input. Value format: - The mask is applied to single-line inputs (it is ignored for `type: multiline`). - For `type: int` and `type: phone`, non-digit characters are removed from the final `value`. - For other single-line types, `value` is usually stored in the same format the user sees (including separators from the mask). Pattern rules: - `0` - any digit - `a` - any letter - `*` - any character - Other characters are treated as fixed - `[]` - make input optional - `{}` - mark fixed characters that should be treated as part of the value (useful for fixed digits like a country code) - `` ` `` - anchors the previous fixed character (like a separator) so it does not shift while the user edits the value Examples: - `+{38}(000)000-00-00` → `+38(097)123-45-67` (fixed country code in `{}`) - 00/`00/`0000 → `12/03/2026` (use `` ` `` to keep separators from shifting while editing) - `000[-000]` → `123` or `123-456` (optional part in `[]`)

value.description:
Integer value.

Notes:
- While the user types, non-digit characters are ignored.
- When not empty, the value is stored as a number.

regexp.description: Validation rule (regular expression). If provided, it becomes the primary validation rule for the field.

length.title: Max length
length.description: Limits the maximum number of characters the user can type.

spellCheck.description: Spellcheck behavior. If omitted, the browser default is used (usually `true`).

regexp.description: Validation rule (regular expression). If provided, it becomes the primary validation rule for the field and overrides built-in validation (including email format validation).

length.title: Max length
length.description: Limits the maximum number of characters the user can type.

removeWhitespace.description: Trim leading and trailing spaces on blur.

Form-grid-one-column.description: A representation of a one column grid

Form-grid-two-column.description: A representation of a two column grid

Page-grid-one-column.description: A representation of a one column grid

Page-grid-two-column.description: A representation of a two column grid

value.description: In **check** and **radio** table modes, two reserved values are available - `genericCheckColumn` and `genericRadioColumn`. Using them you can set the **styleClass** for the header cell, or configure **isSticky** property for the column. Other properties are ignored.

isSticky.title: If true, the column will remain visible in the viewport when table content overflows horizontally
isSticky.description: Makes the column sticky and keeps it visible in the table viewport when horizontal content exceeds the available space. Multiple sticky columns are supported with automatic positioning.

value.title: Value of the row, when table.type = radio

groupValue.title: A group value is used to categorize and group values in the table based on the criteria specified in the extra.groups property

clickable.title: If true, row will be clickable

file.title: File preview
file.description: Displays file preview. `title` is used as `alt` text for images. Optional `label` renders text next to the thumbnail.

label.title: Label next to the image preview

submitOnScroll.title: If true, /send request will be triggered on scroll

page.title: Current page. If present, pagination buttons will be displayed.

rowsPerPageOptions.title: Available number of rows per page

sort.title: Sort settings. If present, table head will be clickable. /send request will be triggered

page.title: Current page. If present clickable pagination buttons will be dispalyed. /send request will be triggered

buttonTitle.title: Title of the button, that will be displayed in each row. /send request will be triggered

sort.title: Sort settings. If present, table head will be clickable. /send request will be triggered

groupButtonTitle.title: Title of the button, that will be used for adding elements in the group

items.description: Flat list of menu nodes that can be reused across the hierarchy.

title.description: Supports bbcode syntax, which will be converted to HTML before rendering.

badge.description: Short badge text that is rendered on the right side of the item.

meta.description:
Arbitrary payload returned in `buttonData.meta` on select or toggle. Accepts any JSON value (object, array, string, number, boolean, or null).

When `extra.postMessageTargetId` is set and meta is an object, the `url` field has a special meaning — it is included in the `postMessage` payload and can be used by the target widget for navigation.

url.description: Navigation URL passed to the target widget in postMessage mode.

structure.title: Hierarchy definition
structure.description:
Mapping of parent IDs to arrays of their children. The special `$root` key is **required** and must list every top-level node.

Example payload:
```json
{
  "$root": ["home", "users", "reports"],
  "users": ["profile", "settings"]
}
```

value.description: Current state of the component that is stored in the form data and sent back to Corezoid with each update.

activeId.description: Identifier of the currently selected node (leaf or branch).

expandedIds.description: Branch IDs that have been expanded inline.

submitOnBranchToggle.description: When true, toggling an inline branch triggers /send with action `expand` or `collapse`.

autoExpandActive.description: Automatically expands all ancestors of the active item. Each change of value.activeId with this flag enabled forces the full path to open.

maxInlineDepth.description: The depth to which branches are rendered inline instead of opening popovers.

highlightActivePath.description: Highlights every node that belongs to the active path.

showPopupTitle.description: Shows the parent item title inside branch popovers.

postMessageTargetId.description: When set, clicking a menu item sends a `postMessage` to the iframe of the target widget instead of submitting a form event to Corezoid. The value must match the `id` of a widget component on the page whose iframe will receive the message.

type.description:
How the `value` property is interpreted.
- `seconds` — value is the initial number of seconds to start counting from.
- `unixtime` — value is a Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch); elapsed time is computed from that moment.

value.description:
Starting value as a string.
- When `type` is `seconds`: the number of seconds to start from (e.g. `"90"` starts at 01:30).
- When `type` is `unixtime`: a Unix timestamp in seconds (e.g. `"1700000000"`); the timer shows elapsed time since that moment.
- Empty string or omitted: starts from 0.

mode.description:
Count direction.
- `up` — stopwatch mode, counts upward from the initial value.
- `down` — countdown mode, counts downward from the initial value. Can go negative if the timer passes zero.

format.description:
Display format.
- `mm:ss` — minutes and seconds; minutes can exceed 59 (e.g. `90:00`).
- `hh:mm:ss` — always shows hours (e.g. `01:30:00`).
- `hh:mm:ss.ms` — includes tenths of a second, updates every 100 ms (e.g. `01:30:00.5`).
- `auto` — shows `hh:mm:ss` when hours > 0, otherwise `mm:ss` (e.g. `03:45` or `01:03:45`).
